description: Moves data from a source location to a destination location. Despite the name, it copies the data; the source remains unchanged. Cannot move memory to memory directly.
Load Effective Address. Calculates the memory address of the source operand and loads it into the destination register. Often used for pointer arithmetic without accessing memory.
Pushes a value onto the top of the stack. Decrements the stack pointer (RSP) by the operand size and stores the operand at the new RSP location.
Pops a value from the top of the stack into a destination. Copies the data at the current stack pointer (RSP) to the destination, then increments RSP.
Adds the source operand to the destination operand, storing the result in the destination.
flags: OF, SF, ZF, AF, CF, PF

description: Subtracts the source operand from the destination operand, storing the result in the destination.
flags: OF, SF, ZF, AF, CF, PF

description: Increments the operand by 1. Often used for loop counters.
flags: OF, SF, ZF, AF, PF (Note: CF is NOT affected)

description: Decrements the operand by 1.
flags: OF, SF, ZF, AF, PF (Note: CF is NOT affected)

description: Compares the destination to the source by performing a subtraction (dest - src), but discards the result. It only updates the CPU flags, which are typically checked by a subsequent jump instruction.
flags: OF, SF, ZF, AF, CF, PF

description: Performs a bitwise AND between the two operands, discarding the result and only updating flags. Often used as 'TEST RAX, RAX' to check if a register is zero.
Unconditional jump. Transfers control to the specified address.
Jump if Equal / Jump if Zero. Jumps to the target if the Zero Flag (ZF) is set to 1.
Jump if Not Equal / Jump if Not Zero. Jumps to the target if the Zero Flag (ZF) is 0.
Jump if Greater (Signed). Jumps if ZF=0 and SF=OF.
Jump if Less (Signed). Jumps if SF != OF.
Calls a procedure. Pushes the return address (the instruction immediately following the CALL) onto the stack, then jumps to the target address.
Returns from a procedure. Pops the return address off the stack and jumps to it.
Performs a bitwise AND operation between destination and source, storing the result in the destination.
Performs a bitwise OR operation between destination and source, storing the result in the destination.
Performs a bitwise Exclusive-OR operation. Often used to clear a register to zero by XORing it with itself (e.g., XOR EAX, EAX).
No Operation. Does absolutely nothing. Often used for padding or alignment.
